title: 8 Best Herbal Remedies for Indigestion and Bloating

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for indigestion because it helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which in turn helps to ease symptoms such as nausea and bloating.
Shogaol helps with indigestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the digestive tract, making it easier to digest food.

Gingerols are good for indigestion because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach, reduce nausea, and ease digestive discomfort, making them a helpful remedy for alleviating symptoms of indigestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger cream, made from Zingiber officinale, is a helpful remedy for indigestion. This is because ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system and ease discomfort. When we eat spicy or heavy foods, it can cause stomach upset and nausea. Ginger cream works by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and reducing inflammation, making it easier to digest food. It also helps to calm the stomach and reduce the production of acid, which can irritate the stomach lining and cause pain. Additionally, ginger cream has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger indigestion. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, are responsible for its beneficial effects on digestion and overall health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream that helps with indigestion, start by gathering some dried Angelica roots and leaves, which can be found at herbal stores or online. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Angelica with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days, to allow the Angelica to infuse its properties into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Then, add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the infused oil, and heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each heating, until the beeswax has melted. Let the mixture cool and thicken, then transfer it to a clean jar and store it in a cool, dark place. This Angelica archangelica herbal cream can be applied topically to the stomach area to help soothe and calm indigestion, and can be stored for up to 6 months in the fridge.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for indigestion

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for indigestion because it helps soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, allowing the digestive system to heal and function properly.
Flavonoids are good for indigestion because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract and reduce irritation and inflammation that can cause discomfort and pain.
Polyphenols are good for indigestion because they help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, which can ease symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is good for indigestion because it has a soothing effect on the stomach. The root of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach. When you have indigestion, your stomach lining can become irritated and inflamed, causing pain and discomfort. The glycyrrhizin in the cream helps to calm this inflammation, making it easier to digest food and reducing the symptoms of indigestion. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra cream also has anti-spasmodic properties, which help to relax the stomach muscles and prevent cramps. This makes it easier to eat and digest food, and can even help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with indigestion.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal creams that help with indigestion, you'll need fresh or dried Angelica roots, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, a double boiler, and a clean glass jar. First, prepare the Angelica by cleaning and drying the roots, then chop them into small pieces. Next, combine the chopped Angelica with the carrier oil in a small saucepan and heat it gently over low heat for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse their properties into the oil. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat, then slowly pour the infused oil into the melted beeswax, stirring constantly. Continue heating the mixture until it reaches a creamy consistency. Pour the cream into the glass jar, let it cool, and it's ready to use as a topical treatment for indigestion. Apply a small amount to the abdomen, gently massaging it in to help soothe digestive discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for indigestion

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Foeniculin is good for indigestion because it helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing food to move through the system more easily and reducing symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
Anethole is good for indigestion because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach, which can help to ease nausea and discomfort associated with indigestion.
Phenolic acids are good for indigestion because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by indigestion.
Coumarins are good for indigestion because they help calm the stomach muscles and reduce inflammation, which can relieve painful cramps and bloating associated with indigestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a natural remedy for indigestion. The active ingredient in fennel creams is anethole, a compound that helps to relax the muscles in the digestive system. When you eat, your stomach muscles contract to mix food with digestive juices. But sometimes, these muscles can contract too much, leading to indigestion symptoms like bloating, cramps, and gas. Fennel creams work by calming down these muscles, allowing food to digest properly and reducing discomfort. This natural relief is especially helpful for people who experience indigestion after eating spicy or fatty foods. By applying fennel cream to the stomach area, you can soothe the digestive system and find quick relief from indigestion symptoms. Regular use of fennel creams may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of indigestion episodes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream for indigestion, first you need to dry the angelica root and leaves. You can air-dry them or use a food dehydrator on a low temperature. Once dry, grind the angelica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix 2 tablespoons of the angelica powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil or other carrier oil in a small bowl. Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the bowl and heat the mixture in a double boiler or a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until the beeswax is melted and the mixture is smooth. Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool slightly, then pour the mixture into a small tin or jar. Let it cool completely before using. To use, apply a small amount to the affected area, such as the abdomen, and gently massage it in. The angelica in the cream may help soothe indigestion and other digestive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for indigestion

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for indigestion because it helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can ease symptoms of indigestion and heartburn.
Rosmarinic acid is good for indigestion because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach, allowing digestive fluids to flow more easily.
Menthone is good for indigestion because it helps to relax the muscles in the digestive system and reduce inflammation, making it easier to digest food and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
Luteolin is good for indigestion because it helps to calm down the stomach and reduce inflammation, making it easier to digest food and relieving symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
Limonene is good for indigestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the digestive system and reduce discomfort and pain caused by indigestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural remedy for indigestion. The active ingredients in peppermint creams, like menthol and menthone, help to relax the muscles in the stomach and ease digestive issues. When you eat, your stomach muscles contract to help break down food, but sometimes these muscles can contract too much, leading to indigestion. The menthol in peppermint creams helps to slow down these muscle contractions, giving your stomach time to digest food properly. This can help to reduce symptoms like bloating, nausea, and stomach pain. Additionally, peppermint creams can also help to improve digestion by increasing the movement of food through the digestive system, which can help to prevent indigestion from happening in the first place.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream for indigestion, you'll need dried Angelica root, coconut oil, beeswax, and a few basic kitchen tools. First, make a strong tea by steeping a tablespoon of dried Angelica root in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then let it cool. Next, combine the cooled tea with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan and gently heat it over low heat until the oil is fully incorporated. Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in 2 tablespoons of beeswax until it's fully melted. Pour the mixture into a small glass jar or tin and let it cool and solidify completely. Once set, your Angelica archangelica cream is ready to use. Simply apply a small amount to the affected area, such as your abdomen, to help soothe indigestion and discomfort. Keep in mind that it's always a good idea to do a patch test before using any new herbal remedy, and consult with a healthcare professional if you have any underlying health conditions or concerns.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for indigestion

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quinine is good for indigestion because it helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing discomfort and inflammation that can cause indigestion symptoms.
Quinic acid is good for indigestion because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of indigestion and discomfort.

Tannins are good for indigestion because they help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, and also slow down the release of digestive enzymes, which can ease symptoms of indigestion and heartburn.

Quinidine is good for indigestion because it helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, allowing food to move through the digestive system more easily and relieving symptoms of indigestion and heartburn.
Alkaloids are good for indigestion because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach and reduce irritation, allowing the digestive system to heal and recover faster.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis cream is a natural remedy used to treat indigestion. The plant's bark has been used for centuries to make a medication called quinine. Quinine helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which can become too tight and cause discomfort. When the muscles relax, food can pass through the digestive system more easily, reducing symptoms of indigestion like bloating and cramps. Cinchona officinalis cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe an irritated digestive tract and reduce inflammation. This can help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause indigestion.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal creams that help with indigestion, start by preparing the dried root of the Angelica plant. You'll need about one tablespoon of the dried root, which can be purchased from a health food store or online. Next, combine the dried root with one cup of boiling water in a saucepan and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to make a strong tea. Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids. Let the tea cool down, then mix it with one tablespoon of beeswax, one tablespoon of coconut oil, and a few drops of peppermint essential oil (optional) in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth and well combined. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the cream cool and thicken before transferring it to a glass jar. Once cooled, the cream can be applied topically to the abdomen to help soothe indigestion and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for indigestion

D-limonene is good for indigestion because it helps to relax the stomach muscles and improve digestion by reducing inflammation and spasms that cause discomfort and pain.

Tannins are good for indigestion because they help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, and also have a soothing effect on the digestive system, allowing it to heal and function properly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Carum carvi, also known as caraway seeds, has been used for centuries to help with indigestion. The cream made from these seeds is a natural remedy that can provide relief from stomach discomfort and bloating. The main active ingredient, carvone, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the stomach muscles and reduce inflammation. This can be especially helpful for people who experience heartburn or acid reflux.  When taken as a cream, the caraway seed extract can be absorbed directly into the skin and then into the bloodstream, where it can target the stomach and intestines to provide relief from indigestion symptoms. This can be a more effective and faster way to get relief compared to taking pills or medications.  The cream also has antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles in the stomach and prevent cramps and spasms. This can be especially helpful for people who experience stomach pain or discomfort after eating.  Using caraway seed cream for indigestion can be a safe and natural alternative to traditional medications. It is also gentle on the stomach and can be used in combination with other treatments to provide relief from indigestion symptoms.  Caraway seed cream can be applied topically to the stomach area, where it can be absorbed directly into the skin and then into the bloodstream. This can be especially helpful for people who experience stomach discomfort or pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal creams that help with indigestion, start by gathering dried root of Angelica archangelica, also known as Wild Celery or Archangel. You'll also need a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. Begin by infusing the dried Angelica root in the carrier oil for a few hours or overnight, allowing the oil to absorb the herb's properties. Strain the oil and discard the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with melted beeswax in a 1:1 ratio and continue heating until the beeswax is fully incorporated. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely, then use it as needed to help soothe indigestion and calm the digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Carum carvi herbal creams for indigestion

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa cream, also known as turmeric cream, is a natural remedy for indigestion. This is because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When we eat spicy or fatty foods, it can cause inflammation in our digestive system, leading to indigestion. Curcumin in turmeric cream helps to reduce this inflammation and soothe the digestive system. It also has a calming effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help to relieve cramps and discomfort. Additionally, turmeric cream has natural antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent the growth of bad bacteria in the digestive system. By reducing inflammation, calming digestive muscles, and preventing bad bacteria, turmeric cream can help to alleviate indigestion symptoms. Turmeric cream is often used with a fatty substance, like coconut oil or ghee, to enhance its absorption and effectiveness in the body.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream that helps with indigestion, you'll need dried Angelica root, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few simple kitchen tools. First, mix 1 tablespoon of dried Angelica root with 2 tablespoons of carrier oil in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the Angelica root is well combined with the oil and the mixture reaches a temperature of around 160°F to 180°F. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly. Next, add 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated. Continue to heat the mixture for a few more minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is melted and the mixture is smooth. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken completely. Once the mixture has cooled, you can transfer it to a clean jar and use it as needed to help soothe indigestion. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to the affected area and massage it in gently.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica cream is made from the root of the angelica plant. This plant has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. The cream is rich in a compound called furanocoumarin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you have indigestion, your stomach and digestive system can become inflamed. This inflammation can cause pain, discomfort, and difficulty eating. The anti-inflammatory properties in the angelica archangelica cream can help to reduce this inflammation and calm the digestive system. The cream can also help to relax the muscles in the stomach and digestive tract, which can become tight and irritated when you have indigestion. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles, the angelica archangelica cream can help to provide relief from indigestion symptoms. The cream can also help to soothe the stomach lining, which can become irritated and inflamed when you have indigestion. This can help to prevent further irritation and reduce the risk of complications.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal cream that helps with indigestion, you'll need dried Angelica roots, coconut oil, beeswax, and a few other simple ingredients. First, steep the dried Angelica roots in a carrier oil like coconut oil for about 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the container every few days. This process is called infusing, and it helps to extract the beneficial properties from the Angelica roots into the oil. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with melted beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is fully incorporated. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring the mixture into small containers or jars. As the mixture cools and solidifies, it will turn into a smooth, creamy herbal cream that can be applied topically to the abdomen to help soothe indigestion and other digestive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Angelica archangelica herbal creams for indigestion

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful remedy for indigestion. Indigestion is a common problem that can cause discomfort, pain, and difficulty eating. If left untreated, it can lead to more serious issues like stomach ulcers and inflammation. Fixing indigestion is essential to improve your quality of life, as it can affect your daily activities, work, and relationships.  Herbal creams containing 'Zingiber officinale', also known as ginger, can help alleviate indigestion. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the stomach. It also helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing food to pass through more smoothly.  Another herb found in some herbal creams is 'Glycyrrhiza glabra', or licorice root. Licorice root has been traditionally used to soothe stomach ulcers and reduce inflammation. It also has antibacterial properties that can prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the stomach, which can cause indigestion.  Using herbal creams to treat indigestion can be beneficial as they can be applied topically to the stomach area, allowing the herbs to be absorbed directly into the skin. This can be a more effective and faster way to relieve indigestion symptoms compared to taking oral medications. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any new remedy, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.
supplementary_best: To remedy indigestion, the best combination of herbal creams is a blend of Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Carum carvi. Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, soothes the stomach with its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing bloating and cramps. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which helps to calm the digestive system and ease indigestion. Carum carvi, also known as caraway, has a carminative effect, helping to release trapped gas and alleviate bloating. When combined, these three herbs create a powerful and natural remedy for indigestion. The soothing properties of fennel and licorice root work together to calm the stomach, while caraway's carminative properties help to release trapped gas and alleviate bloating. This combination is particularly effective because it addresses multiple aspects of indigestion, making it a comprehensive and reliable remedy.
supplementary_avoid: If you have indigestion, it's best to avoid herbal creams that contain peppermint and sage. Peppermint can cause stomach muscles to contract, making indigestion worse. Sage can also irritate the stomach and make digestion problems worse. Another herb to avoid is licorice root, which can slow down digestion and make indigestion symptoms last longer. Another one to avoid is dandelion root, which can increase stomach acid production, making indigestion worse. These herbs can be found in some herbal creams, so it's essential to read the ingredient list carefully before using them. It's also crucial to talk to your doctor or a healthcare professional before using any herbal creams, especially if you're already taking medication for indigestion.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera juice can help soothe indigestion by calming the stomach lining. This is because aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract. Drinking aloe vera juice after eating can help ease heartburn and acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, ginger has natural anti-inflammatory compounds called gingerols, which can help ease digestion and reduce nausea. Ginger can be consumed in the form of tea, powder, or raw ginger, and it can be added to food for flavor. Probiotics are also beneficial for indigestion as they help maintain a healthy balance of good bacteria in the gut, which can aid in digestion and prevent the growth of bad bacteria that can cause indigestion. Apple cider vinegar has also been found to help alleviate indigestion symptoms by balancing the body's pH levels and aiding digestion. The acidity in apple cider vinegar can help stimulate the digestive system and promote the breakdown of food in the stomach.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with indigestion often experience other ailments like bloating, gas, acid reflux, and stomach pain. They may also have heartburn, nausea, and vomiting. Medicinal herbs can help heal these conditions. Ginger, for example, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. It also helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which can ease bloating and gas. Chamomile tea can also calm the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can help with heartburn and acid reflux. Dandelion root has natural digestive enzymes that can help break down food and reduce stomach pain. Licorice root can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, which can help with ulcers and stomach irritation. These herbs work by reducing inflammation, soothing the stomach, and improving digestion, which can help alleviate the symptoms of indigestion and related ailments.
